raw_consumer_statement:
Hello, I recently used my AMEX Everyday Visa card for 2 rentals in the month of XXXX. AMEX charged me {$24.00} per rental for premium insurance which I did not recall signing up for. When I called AMEX to report these charges they said I signed up for it in XXXX which if it was true was done deceptively. 
I made two calls to AMEX 1 ) On XX/XX/XXXX to notify AMEX about the charges. The agent said she will create a ticket and refund the amount within a week and I will receive a letter. 
When I didn't see the amount refunded and neither a letter I called back on XX/XX/XXXX and spoke to a supervisor by the name of XXXX who mentioned that AMEX will not be refunding this amount since the rentals were consumed. 
This is a gross unethical practice by AMEX by using a deceptive method to get customers to sign up for premium insurance 12 years ago and then not notifying them. I have requested copy of the confirmation of my signup from XXXX but haven't received one after multiple calls. 
I would really appreciate if CFPB could look into these practices by AMEX.
